text: **Anna:** Today, Pete and I are meeting with a {consultant|相談役/顧問|/kənˈsʌltənt/|A business consultant helps companies improve.} who will help us with our new show.

**Professor Bot:** You know the past tense, right?

**Professor Bot:** When we talk about two things in the past, we can use the past perfect for the first event.
**Anna:** Put had before the past participle.
**Anna:** Pete had {promised|約束した|/prəˈmɪst/|I promised to call my friend.} he would meet Anna.
**Anna:** Here's your {assignment|課題/任務|/əˈsaɪnmənt/|Complete your homework assignment.}

**Anna:** Sure.
